data.title: debug-everything Bug-Fix Mode: orchestrator reverts to manual investigation after auto-compact instead of resuming fix-bug protocol
data.description:
## Actual Behavior
After an auto-compact event mid-session, the debug-everything orchestrator resumed by manually reading test files and searching for root causes through direct code analysis (count_centrality function, grep behavior, bash version differences) instead of following the fix-bug skill protocol (Step 1.5: intent search → hypothesis loop → RED test → fix).

## Expected Behavior
After context compaction, the orchestrator should re-enter the fix-bug skill at the appropriate phase (investigation), following the structured investigation → hypothesis → RED test → fix pipeline. Manual troubleshooting replaces structured gate checks with undisciplined exploration.

## Rationalization (for record)
The agent rationalized drift by claiming it was still in the 'investigation phase' of fix-bug, but investigation in fix-bug has structure: form hypotheses, confirm/disprove with targeted code reads, then write RED tests. The unstructured approach involved reading 15+ test sections sequentially, checking multiple potential failure modes without committing to any hypothesis, and running the test locally (26/26 pass) without having a specific Ubuntu-only hypothesis to test. This is manual debugging, not the fix-bug workflow.

## Contributing Factor
Auto-compact events reset the skill context. The session summary contained enough information to resume fix-bug at Step 5 (write RED test) or Step 4.5 (complexity evaluation), but the orchestrator re-entered at Step 1 (investigation from scratch) without invoking the fix-bug skill framework. The ORCHESTRATOR_RESUME guidance in fix-bug SKILL.md exists specifically to prevent this.
